macro_segments.title: Food Edibility and Chametz Status: Moldy Bread and the Dog Standard

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah on moldy bread edible to dogs but not humans
The principle of status stickiness: retaining food classification
Dispute between Rav Natan and the Sages on loss of food status
The Chachamim's position: inedible even to dogs loses food status
Application to Pesach: medicines, toothpaste, and subjective importance

macro_segments.title: Chametz in Leather-Worker Troughs: When Processing Renders It Inedible

micro_segments.title: The case of flour placed in leather-worker troughs for leather treatment
Rabbi Natan's position: leather placement removes obligation to remove chametz
Rava's ruling: even one day or one hour of leather placement exempts

macro_segments.title: Dough in Cracks and Chatzitzah: Objective vs. Subjective Criteria

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah: dough in trough cracks as part of vessel or obstruction
Distinction between Pesach (size-based) and other times (intent-based) criteria
Rav Yehuda's challenge: different standards apply differently
Abaye's reinterpretation: the issue is combining food, not dunking in mikva
The apple and dough case: combining small quantities for tumah purposes
Rava's alternative reading: the Mishnah addresses mikva immersion, not combining
The excluded middle: what if you neither want nor don't want it there
Rav Pappa's objection: the Mishnah speaks of tumah, not taharah
Rav Pappa's answer: whether the vessel becomes tamei from touching the sheretz via the dough

macro_segments.title: Hardened Dough: Determining Chametz Status Without Observation

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah on dough too hardened to observe fermentation
Determining fermentation by comparison to other doughs made at same time
When no comparison doughs exist: the eighteen-minute standard based on walking distance
The mil measurement: time to walk from Migdal Nunia to Tverya

macro_segments.title: Quantifying Halakhic Time: The Four-Mil Standard for Various Obligations

micro_segments.title: Rav Abbahu citing Resh Lakish on four mils for kneading dough in purity
Prayer timing: four mils to reach a synagogue for davening
Washing hands: four mils to reach water before eating
Leather processing: four mils of walking on hides for tanning
Rabbi Yosi's qualification: four-mil rule applies only in direction of travel, not backtracking

macro_segments.title: Separating Challah on Yom Tov When It Becomes Tamei

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah's problem: disposing of tamei challah on Yom Tov
Rabbi Eliezer's solution: delay separation until after baking
Ben Beteira's solution: preserve dough in cold water until after Yom Tov
Rabbi Yehoshua's solution: the challah is sanctified and not your property
Debate on tovat hana'ah: does the right to distribute constitute ownership
Rabbi Eliezer vs. Rabbi Yehoshua on reversibility and hypothetical ownership

macro_segments.title: Cooking on Yom Tov: Intention vs. Theoretical Possibility

micro_segments.title: The case of baking on Yom Tov explicitly for the next day
Rav Chisda's position: you need actual intention for Yom Tov benefit
Rabbah's position: theoretical possibility of benefit suffices (ho'il principle)
The challenge: cooking from Yom Tov for Shabbat with Eiruv Tavshilin
Rabbah's justification: Yom Tov and Shabbat share one kedusha when contiguous
The sick animal case: slaughtering on Yom Tov only if time to roast and eat
Rav Chisda's objection: how can financial loss override Torah prohibition
Rabbah's resolution: financial loss justifies allowing the act if intention is genuine

topical_tags.term: Ra'ui la'achilas kelev (fit for dog consumption)
Tzorech Yom Tov (for purpose of Yom Tov)
Eiruv Tavshilin (special preparation for cooking from Yom Tov to Shabbat)
